name: task-lifecycle-three-skill-trio
tags: task lifecycle, task-grooming, task-implementation, task-introspection, task-framework, skills, execution loop, task:de6b2945
body:
The task lifecycle is now three companion skills, each independently invocable but designed to run in sequence: /task-grooming (pre-implementation, reduce uncertainty) → /task-implementation (execution-loop behavioral guide while working, cross-referenced from /task-framework step 4) → /task-introspection (post-close retrospective, evolve the system). All three share the same "judgment over syntax" philosophy established 2026-07-05.

Why: completes a session-long effort merging the user's judgment-first redesigns of grooming and introspection, then adding the missing middle piece (implementation) to close the loop.

How to apply: when any of the three needs future revision, check whether the change should ripple into the other two for consistency (e.g. a new engineering-judgment question, a new warning sign, a new cross-reference). They're not independent artifacts — they're one lifecycle split into three phases.
